observations.observation: codex_consult.status = "failed_hook_block" is not in the documented enum (ok | failed_quota | failed_timeout | failed_parse | not_requested). This is a report-schema protocol issue, not a file-organization cleanliness issue. The feedback_summary field accurately documents the hook-block cause and self-review substitution. Out of cleanliness-inspector scope.

Entry count changed from 489 to 522 in working tree (+33 entries). All new entries verified to resolve to files on disk. The staged-only diff shows 489 to 510 (+21). No missing or phantom file references found. No cleanliness issue.

detect_orphan_agents.note: Pre-existing finding; product-owner.md is not in the changed files set. introduced_in_diff: false.

detect_orphan_scripts.note: Multiple pre-existing orphan scripts found in scripts/todo/ (35–72 days old) and scripts/install/. None are in the changed files set. introduced_in_diff: false for all.

detect_duplicate_content.note: Duplicate content found only in worktrees/ and projects/ (session caches). None in the changed files set. introduced_in_diff: false for all.

summary.verdict: CLEAN — no cleanliness issues introduced by this diff

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 2 issues and 2 observations. Issue 1: the failed_hook_block status finding is a report-schema issue, not a cleanliness issue — reclassified to observation_only and no cleanliness finding raised. Issue 2: INDEX.md count discrepancy noted (draft said +30 based on staged diff; working tree shows +33); corrected in report. OBSERVATION_ONLY items 3 and 4 were logged without changes required.

findings.issue: Finding 1 (failed_hook_block) is a report-schema issue, not a cleanliness/file-organization issue. Counting it as the sole cleanliness finding is a false positive for cleanliness scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to OBSERVATION_ONLY: docs/dev/dev-report-20260524-123039.json uses non-standard codex_consult.status='failed_hook_block'; this is a report-schema issue, not a cleanliness issue. No cleanliness finding is raised.
findings.rationale: Correct — cleanliness-inspector scope is file organization, not JSON field conformance. Finding downgraded to advisory observation_only; total_issues set to 0.

findings.issue: INDEX count claim was stale/inaccurate. git diff HEAD shows 489→522 (+33 entries) in working tree; staged-only shows 489→510.
findings.proposed_fix: Recompute against exact diff and state the correct count.
findings.rationale: Observation section now states the working-tree count (+33) while noting staged-only count (+21). Both are accurate for their respective scopes.

findings.issue: No proposed deletion/archive action appears in the draft, so no Safety Rules violation around deleting/relocating files modified <7 days.
Final cleanliness report should include mandatory script JSON outputs or evidence references.
findings.rationale: Script outputs included in mandatory_script_outputs section above.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied both issues with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) reclassified failed_hook_block to observation_only (not a cleanliness finding); (2) corrected INDEX entry count to reflect working tree diff. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY items logged without changes.
